name: ensembl-database
description: Query Ensembl genome database REST API for 250+ species. Gene lookups, sequence retrieval, variant analysis, comparative genomics, orthologs, VEP predictions, for genomic research.
capabilities: Look up gene information by symbol or Ensembl ID for 250+ species
Retrieve DNA, transcript, and protein sequences from genomic databases
Analyze genetic variants using the Variant Effect Predictor (VEP)
Find orthologs and paralogs across species for comparative genomics
Convert coordinates between genome assemblies (GRCh37 to GRCh38)
Query regulatory features and phenotype associations
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: All 177 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate bioinformatics tool querying the Ensembl genome database (maintained by EMBL-EBI). Scanner misidentified GA4GH Beacon API endpoints as Cobalt Strike keywords, HTTP status codes as cryptographic algorithms, and markdown code fences as shell command execution. Network requests target public bioinformatics APIs with proper rate limiting.
valueStatement: Query the Ensembl genome database REST API for vertebrate genomic data. Look up genes, retrieve sequences, analyze variants with VEP, and perform comparative genomics across 250+ species.
limitations: Rate limited to 15 requests per second for anonymous users
Only queries Ensembl REST API; does not access local databases
Returns raw API responses; downstream analysis requires additional processing

useCases.title: Gene Annotation Pipeline
useCases.description: Look up genes, retrieve transcripts, get protein sequences, find orthologs, and export results for analysis.

useCases.title: Variant Analysis
useCases.description: Query variants by rsID, predict functional consequences with VEP, check population frequencies, retrieve phenotype associations.

useCases.title: Comparative Genomics
useCases.description: Find orthologs across species, retrieve sequences, compare gene structures, analyze evolutionary conservation.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Gene Lookup
promptTemplates.prompt: Look up the gene TP53 in human using Ensembl and return its Ensembl ID, chromosomal location, and description.

promptTemplates.title: Sequence Retrieval
promptTemplates.prompt: Retrieve the DNA sequence for the BRCA2 gene (ENSG00000139618) in FASTA format.

promptTemplates.title: Variant Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the variant rs699 using VEP and show its predicted consequences and population frequencies.

promptTemplates.title: Cross-Species Comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all mouse orthologs for human gene BRCA2 and show their Ensembl IDs and sequence similarity.

bestPractices: Respect the 15 requests per second rate limit for anonymous users
Use batch endpoints when querying multiple items to improve efficiency
Cache frequently accessed data to reduce API calls
antiPatterns: Making sequential single requests instead of using batch endpoints
Ignoring rate limit responses (429 status codes)
Not specifying the correct assembly (GRCh37 vs GRCh38) for coordinate queries

faq.question: What species are supported?
faq.answer: Ensembl covers 250+ vertebrate species including human, mouse, zebrafish, and fruit fly.

faq.question: How do I handle rate limits?
faq.answer: Implement retry logic with the Retry-After header. Maximum 15 requests per second for anonymous users.

faq.question: What is VEP?
faq.answer: Variant Effect Predictor analyzes genetic variants and predicts their functional consequences on genes and proteins.

faq.question: How do I query older genome assemblies?
faq.answer: Use https://grch37.rest.ensembl.org for GRCh37/hg19 queries. Current assemblies use https://rest.ensembl.org.

faq.question: Can I batch multiple queries?
faq.answer: Yes, use POST endpoints with JSON arrays to query multiple IDs in a single request.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Most endpoints support JSON, FASTA for sequences, and some support XML or plain text.
